title: Intake gate exempts git when everything it would change is bookkeeping (D1, D3, D4) — closes P46 / GH #1
action: Per CONTEXT D1/D3/D4 — this is backlog P46, open since v1.3.10, whose own text predicted the incident: 'the intake gate blocks git once phase is terminal, forcing workarounds'. In the intake-gate branch of `checkWrite` (skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/guards.mjs — the canonical source; the hook shim only dispatches into it): (1) READ-ONLY git is ALWAYS allowed regardless of phase (status, log, diff, show, rev-parse, branch --list, tag --list, remote -v, ls-files, check-ignore, merge-base, rev-list, describe, blame, cat-file — enumerate deliberately, default to REFUSING an unrecognized subcommand rather than guessing it is read-only). (2) A MUTATING git command (commit, add, push, tag, merge, rm, mv, checkout, restore, reset, stash, ...) is allowed while the phase is terminal ONLY IF every path it would change is inside the gate's existing writable-without-routing set (.bee/, docs/, plans/, AGENTS.md). Compute eligibility from the ACTUAL staged/target paths at check time (D4) — `git diff --cached --name-only` for a staged commit, the explicit pathspecs for `git add`/`rm`, etc. — NEVER from the command's wording, a flag, or an env var: `git commit -m "just bookkeeping"` over a source file must still refuse. A single source path in the set keeps today's refusal verbatim. (3) D3: rewrite the refusal's FIX line so it names the bookkeeping-only exemption and the ordinary route FIRST (commit bookkeeping BEFORE closing the phase), and mentions `guards.idle_gate` last as a repo-level opt-out — the current message points the operator at disabling the guard, which is exactly how the incident happened. Open question resolved conservatively: `git push` gets NO exemption (outward-facing) — state that in the message. RED-FIRST: checks first in hooks/test_write_guard.mjs, run red, record --passed false, then implement. Checks: terminal phase + staged only .bee/ and docs/ -> commit ALLOWED; terminal phase + one staged source file -> commit REFUSED with today's semantics; `git status`/`git log` allowed at any phase; an unrecognized git subcommand is refused, not assumed safe; a misleading commit MESSAGE never changes the outcome; push still refused at terminal phase; and the refusal text names the safe route before the opt-out. Mirror to .bee/bin, render + manifest, TARGETED verify only (the write-guard suite).

truths: read-only git allowed at any phase; unrecognized git subcommand refused rather than assumed read-only
mutating git allowed at a terminal phase ONLY when every changed path is bookkeeping; one source path restores today's refusal
eligibility computed from actual paths, never from wording/flags/env — a misleading -m message changes nothing
git push stays refused at a terminal phase
the refusal FIX line names the safe route first and guards.idle_gate last
RED-FIRST failing run recorded before implementation
prohibitions: Never let a source-file change through the terminal-phase gate
No env var or flag may widen the exemption
Fail closed on anything the parser cannot classify

trace.outcome: Intake gate exempts git only when every path it would change is bookkeeping; read-only git always allowed with fail-closed on unknown subcommands; push never exempt; refusal now names the safe route first. Closes P46 / GH #1
trace.verify_output:
ok    - row49: stderr identifies the intake gate
ok    - row49: stderr names the actual offending source path
ok    - row50: a `-m "just bookkeeping"` message does NOT change the outcome — still refused (D4)
ok    - row51: `git push` is refused at a terminal phase (no exemption)
ok    - row51: stderr says push is never exempted
ok    - row52: an unrecognized git subcommand (`git bisect`) is refused, not assumed safe
ok    - row53: the FIX line names the bookkeeping/bee-hive route before guards.idle_gate (D3)
ok    - row54: `git add` with an explicit source pathspec is refused at a terminal phase
ok    - row55: `git add` with an explicit bookkeeping pathspec is allowed at a terminal phase
ok    - row56: `git commit` with a staged source path is unaffected outside a terminal phase (scope control)

ALL PASS
